examined: Upheld ADR-001/master-worker split: the PR keeps the master/metadata authority untouched and adds client-side switching through existing worker/service-discovery APIs; evidence /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:316, /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:387

Upheld availability/elasticity goals in PRD and arc42: URMA data-plane degradation triggers worker switching while current worker remains usable when no candidate exists; evidence /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:320, /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:387, /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:1483

Upheld performance-first transport model: URMA remains the fast path, TCP fallback continues business traffic, and the new tracker is request-driven with min sample/window/backoff gates rather than adding blocking coordination; evidence /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:252, /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:272, /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:448

Upheld config validation convention: new gflags use DS_DEFINE macros and registered validators with documented client config behavior; evidence /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:21, /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:740

Upheld test expectations for failover and concurrency risk: UT covers tracker state/concurrency/backoff and ST covers remote failover, TCP fallback, switch failure keeping current worker, dynamic config, and local switchback; evidence /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:854, /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:1652
